title: What lies behind the happiness facade? | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 1.13.2 | 19-01-2020
speaker: HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

segments.text: Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 1, Chapter 13, and Text 2.
Jatayika Bhaktir Govinde Tebhyas Chopra Ramah
Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shri Prabhupada
After asking various questions and becoming established in the transcendental loving service of Lord Krishna,
Vidura retired from putting questions to Maitreya Muni.
Vidura retired from putting questions before Maitreya Muni when he was convinced by Maitreya Rishi
that the Sanmam Bonam of life,
is to be finally situated in the transcendental loving service of Lord Shri Krishna,
who is Govinda or one who satisfies His devotees in all respects.
The conditioned soul, the living being in material existence,
seeks happiness by employing his senses in the modes of materialism,
but that cannot give him satisfaction.
He then searches after the supreme truth by the empiric-philosophic speculative method,

and by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth, by the supreme truth.
But if he doesn't find the ultimate goal,
he again goes down to material activities
and engages himself in various philanthropic and altruistic works,
which all fail to give him satisfaction.
So, neither fructive activities nor dry philosophical speculation can give one satisfaction,
because by nature, a living being is the eternal servitor of the Supreme Lord Shri Krishna.
give him direction towards that ultimate end. The Bhagavad Gita confirms this statement.
Like Vidura, an inquisitive conditioned soul must approach a bona fide spiritual master
like Maitreya and by intelligent inquiries must try to know everything about karma,
fruitive activities, jnana, philosophical research for the supreme truth, and yoga,
the linking process of spiritual realization. One who is not seriously inclined to put questions
before a spiritual master need not accommodate a show bottle spiritual
master, nor should a person who may be a spiritual master for others pose to be so
if he is unable to engage his disciple, ultimately in the transcendental loving service of Lord
Sri Krishna. Vidura was successful in approaching such a spiritual
Master like Maitreya and he got the ultimate goal of life, Bhakti unto Govinda. Thus, there was
nothing to be known further about spiritual progress. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Bhagavata.
We are reading from the chapter, Dhritarashtra quits home. And here,
till this verse, the conversation between a great devotee by name Vidura
and Maitreya Rishi was described. And in the last verse, Atmano Gathim,
the destination for the Self or the Soul was discussed. And here, it is said that
Yavatah Krutavan Prashnan, Vidura asked all these questions.
And uh jatayika bhakti govinde. When he understood that Atmana Gathi is
ekabhakti, ekabhakti govinde, the unalloyed devotional service to Lord Shri Krishna.
Then Tebhyescha Upararama, he has retired from asking questions.

But Prabhupada here is talking about one more deeper aspect. He's talking about satisfaction.
Externally, people can look like they are happy but when it comes to satisfaction, that
is a different kind of a situation. So, Prabhupada is saying, although everybody is trying to
enjoy in the materialism, but nobody is happy, nobody is satisfied. Without satisfaction,
happiness is not possible. Satisfaction is required. So, they did one survey of the big

that I have not committed suicide, but then I was so lonely. And then I am grateful that
I have not committed suicide, but then I was so lonely that all these thoughts were
crossing my mind. Now, if you see all these people, either in the movies or TVs and newspapers,
